framing: Candidate barriers/drivers with evidence strength — HYPOTHESES, not conclusions. To be screened by survey and refined by interviews.
executive_summary: Grocery dominance and routine habit-lock leave 87.9% of user feedback devoid of non-grocery cross-category exploration signals.
Catalog assortment gaps (17.3%) and friction in app discovery (13.3%) represent the primary blockers preventing motivated users from trying new categories.
Perceived price markups and quality skepticism for fragile or high-value items push users back to specialist offline stores and traditional e-commerce.
Acute emergency needs (such as late-night printing, pet care, or immediate gadget replacement) act as the leading organic catalyst for accidental category discovery.

category_opportunities.barrier: High trust deficit regarding defective products, scalping perception, and rigid non-return policies.
category_opportunities.opportunity: Introduce instant 10-minute replacement guarantees and visible warranty badging to capture urgent, high-margin gadget needs.

category_opportunities.barrier: Difficulty discovering curated budget products and privacy/discretion concerns during delivery.
category_opportunities.opportunity: Create under-₹500 curated gift/trial bundles and discreet packaging options at checkout.

category_opportunities.barrier: Perceived excessive delivery/handling charges on bulky items and low consumer awareness of general utility stock.
category_opportunities.opportunity: Offer threshold-based handling fee waivers on household utility bundles (e.g., packing supplies, home hardware).

category_opportunities.barrier: Low organic awareness that pet supplies exist on the platform until an emergency occurs.
category_opportunities.opportunity: Target pet owners with automated repeat-replenishment triggers and dedicated pet essential banners during morning peak hours.

recommended_experiments.hypothesis: Displaying relevant non-grocery impulse add-ons (e.g., stationery printout service during exam season) on the cart screen will increase cross-category trial by 15%.
Adding explicit 'Instant 10-Minute Return/Exchange' trust badges on high-value electronics and fragile items will boost add-to-cart rates by 20%.
Replacing blank zero-result search screens with intelligent category recommendations or similar alternatives will increase cross-category exploration by 12%.
Displaying unit-price comparison indicators against offline stores on home and general merchandise will lower price-uncertainty drop-offs by 10%.

surprising_insights: Emergency utility requirements (such as urgent late-night exam admit card printouts or packing supplies) drive higher-intent cross-category discovery than standard promotional discounts.

The 'convenience_first' segment exhibits high duality, comprising both the largest routine-locked cohort (26) and the largest explorer cohort (21), indicating that convenience triggers switch rapidly between rigid lock-in and spontaneous category expansion.
Social awkwardness and packaging discretion directly impact personal care discovery; users express hesitation when sensitive wellness items are delivered in non-discreet packaging.

locked_in_segments.traits: High-frequency buyers purchasing daily staples, fresh produce, and milk; highly resistant to navigating beyond the primary grocery feed.
Prioritizes speed and immediate gratification; routinely buys familiar top-up items unless driven by an acute emergency.
Constantly cross-checks prices against local kirana stores and competitor apps; easily deterred by delivery fees and perceived markups.

explorer_segments.traits: Leverages rapid 10-minute delivery to solve urgent real-world problems across diverse non-grocery items.
Actively browses high-value electronics, seasonal home goods, and niche food items for consolidated single-order convenience.
Uses the platform for time-sensitive celebrations, surprise doorstep deliveries, and holiday-specific items.

top_jobs_to_be_done.job: Fulfill daily household and fresh grocery needs rapidly
Procure emergency gadgets, electronics, and accessories on demand
Grab quick snack and beverage top-ups
Source routine daily dairy items reliably
Obtain personal care and beauty items discreetly and quickly

unmet_needs: Guaranteed temperature-controlled cold chain delivery (e.g., ice cream arriving unmelted without requiring removed ice packs).
Deeper inventory variety and specialized brand availability for niche groceries and stationery.
Transparent pricing without extra handling or bulk fees on heavy household goods.
Clear return, exchange, and customer support accountability for defective electronics or apparel sizing.
Improved in-app search capabilities that parse generic or descriptive queries rather than exact keyword matches.

powerful_quotes.quote: Fed the cat some baby cat food

hypotheses.hypothesis: Catalog assortment gaps and missing specialized SKUs actively force motivated users to churn back to specialist offline markets or traditional e-commerce platforms.
hypotheses.rationale: Assortment gaps account for 17.3% of relevant feedback (the largest barrier), with users frequently encountering out-of-stock items or missing specialty brands when attempting to expand purchases beyond staple groceries.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Uncertainty surrounding quick-commerce price markups and added handling fees creates severe friction for price-conscious shoppers considering non-grocery categories.
hypotheses.rationale: Price uncertainty (11.6%) and higher costs compared to offline alternatives (5.2%) combine to create reluctance among users comparing quick commerce against local markets, DMart, or Zepto.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Skepticism around product quality, cold-chain integrity, and lack of return accountability deters users from purchasing high-value electronics, fragile goods, or perishable dairy.
hypotheses.rationale: Quality distrust represents 11.0% of feedback, driven by negative experiences such as melted ice creams, damaged milk packaging, and unresolved support tickets for defective electronics.

hypotheses.hypothesis: Friction in app navigation and keyword-rigid search capabilities hide non-grocery verticals, keeping users unaware of broader catalog offerings.
hypotheses.rationale: In-app discovery issues represent 13.3% of mentions, backed by explicit user quotes detailing difficulties finding specialty categories like stationery or gift items within the app hierarchy.
